nodes.label: index.tsx

securityFindings.title: Potential SQL injection
securityFindings.description: String concatenation in SQL queries can lead to SQL injection
securityFindings.code: alert(`Can't delete this tier. ${count} user(s) are assigned to it. Reassign them first.`)
securityFindings.explanation: This appears to be a false positive. The code shows a client-side alert message with string interpolation, not SQL query construction. However, if 'count' comes from user input without validation, it could potentially be used for XSS attacks if this string is ever rendered as HTML.
securityFindings.fix: Ensure the 'count' variable is properly validated as a number and consider using template literals with proper escaping if this message will be displayed in HTML context. For actual SQL injection prevention, use parameterized queries or prepared statements.

executiveSummary.headline: React TypeScript application with admin functionality and modular component architecture
executiveSummary.architecture: This is a React-based web application built with TypeScript, organized into a standard modular structure with separate clusters for components, hooks, services, and pages. The application features an admin interface as a key entry point and follows modern React patterns with custom hooks and service layers for business logic separation.
strengths: Strong TypeScript adoption for type safety
Well-organized modular architecture with clear separation of concerns
concerns: Significant dead code presence (22 items) indicating maintenance issues
Security vulnerability detected requiring immediate attention
